stem: At a workshop, there are a total of 450 participants. Each participant is assigned to either team A, team B, or team C. If one of these participants is selected at random, the probability of selecting a participant assigned to team A is 0.30 and the probability of selecting a participant assigned to team B is 0.50. How many participants are assigned to team C?
rationale: The correct answer is 90. The three probabilities must sum to 1, so P(C) = 1 - 0.30 - 0.50 = 0.20. Therefore the number of participants on team C is 0.20 × 450 = 90.
